As I go and everyone in this video, we're going to cover how to get a Twilio account set up and also how to get your number. Now Twilio is really important for most applications that we use because it gives you a centralized number that's not tied to a specific phone. That means we can later associate this with other software that where you're able to have other people manage it or even set up automations for it. We're going to include a link below this video to twilio.com directly to the same sign up page. You should see the same twilio.com slash try dash twilio in your address bar when you click on that link. It should take you right here. Now if they update the page, it may look a bit different, but the process should be pretty much the same. But type in your name and you do want to use real information. If you don't use real information, there's a chance it can flag your account and that wouldn't be a good thing. You have to go through verification at that. Now they do have a 14 character requirement for the password, so make sure you choose a password that's long enough. You always want to use something unique, nothing with your name or email address in it that way nobody's able to guess it. Couldn't put a check mark next to I am not a robot. And sometimes you may see these little verifications, these captures. Pass that one. Luckily I'm not a robot. We're going to go ahead and hit this red get started button now. Now just to keep in mind when you click the button you are agreeing to their legal policies. So if you have any questions, if you're unsure, always read what you're signing up for. I've already read it before. I'm okay with agreeing it. So I'm clicking that red get started. Now it does want you to enter a phone number to verify and I'm going to enter in a number in here. And the reason why they want you to is they want to verify you're a real person and they're also going to send you a verification code. So if you don't have a real phone handy that you can verify right away, you'll want to pause and go get that phone that you want to use for the verification. Now also if you don't want them to contact you about free onboarding and setup, you want to put a check mark there. So putting a check mark there means that they are not going to contact you about doing the onboarding process. I'm familiar with Twilio and I don't feel that I need it. So I'm putting the check mark there. It's up to you. I'm going to go ahead and hit this verify and let's start the verification process. And so they just sent me an SMS, a text message to my phone. So I'm going to go and see if I got that code and I didn't. And your code is going to be different than mine. If you didn't get the code or you want to use a phone call, it gives you that link right there but I did get the code. So I'm going to go ahead and hit the submit there. Now that we verified it, it's going to give you a customized your experience. So you can go through this if you want. It kind of gives them a way to gauge what your experience level is. That way you're able to really work with whatever they offer. They give this welcome to Twilio message. I'm going to hit OK. That way we don't have to see it. And they do give you a trial balance. Now you do want to go ahead and add in a credit card at some point, upgrade the account, but I'm not going to show you that process just because it does require entering in a credit card. But when you're ready upgrade, you click upgrade right there. You're able to enter in your payment information. Since we're already verified, we don't have to do that step. And that asks you how much you want to add to the account. If you do automatic recharge, and that's probably a good thing to do, it's going to automatically bill you to get your balance back up to $20 if ever you're below $10. That means you never have to worry about being short on your account. So once you've upgraded your account, let's get your number. So I'm going to click this get a trial number. You can actually search for a different number if you want to. So if I didn't like that number, I can go and search for a different one. You can enter in the country and you can start putting in the numbers that you want it to be. So if I want 512888 to match the first part of the number or anywhere in the number or last part, I can choose that. And at the very least, you want to set it up to be able to do voice, SMS, and MMS. Or you can just leave that set to any. Any means it's able to do all four of those. I don't need fax, so it's an optional one for me. But I'm going to leave it any just in case I do want to be able to fax from that number later on. Go ahead and go and hit search. And if it has any matches, it'll let you know. Otherwise it'll give you this. I'm just going to type in some random numbers, see if we can find anything that it does have a match for. And here we go. It gave us a few different numbers here and you see it tells you what the capabilities are. Once you find the number you'd like, go ahead and choose this number. If you don't like these, you can refresh the results or go back and revise it. You may take a couple moments, that's okay, but it lets you know that your new phone number is created. You're going to go ahead and hit done. And that's it. So I now have my phone number set up. So just to kind of review the process, you go to Twilio, you get signed up with an account, verify your account as part of that. You want to upgrade your account by adding billing information and then you want to get your number. Once you've done all that, your Twilio account's ready to use and you're good to go for the next step. I hope you enjoyed this video and have a great rest of your night.
